meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Students: Solve Debugging Fatigue with Smart SaaS Pricing
content.seo_description: Learn to overcome persistent coding bugs by implementing smart SaaS pricing models. An intermediate case study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Debugging frustration often stems from technical debt incurred by unclear product scope. By implementing a tiered SaaS pricing model, you force scope discipline. Use Claude Code to audit your codebase and Antigravity to architect a pricing-aware logic layer, transforming your project from a buggy prototype into a professional, sustainable SaaS.
content.intro:
The screen flickers, the console logs scream red, and you are staring at a recursive error that seems to have no end. For a student developer, this is the 'Valley of Despair'—where passion projects go to die because the code has become a tangled web of unmanageable logic. You feel like you are chasing ghosts, unable to debug because your feature creep has outpaced your architecture.

But what if the solution wasn't just better coding, but better business logic? By framing your application around a structured SaaS pricing model, you force yourself to define clear feature boundaries. This transition from 'build everything' to 'ship value' is the secret weapon that separates hobbyists from professional software engineers.

steps.title: Define Feature Gates via Pricing Tiers
steps.description: Map your current feature set to three tiers: Free, Pro, and Enterprise. This limits the scope of what needs to be debugged in each module.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase structure and suggest a feature-gating architecture that maps existing functions to three distinct SaaS pricing tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'map existing functions to pricing tiers and create a feature-flag configuration file'

steps.title: Implement Middleware for Access Control
steps.description: Centralize your logic so that features are only accessible if the user's subscription tier permits. This reduces surface area for bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust middleware pattern in TypeScript that checks user subscription status before executing protected controller methods.

steps.title: Automate Error Reporting by Tier
steps.description: Prioritize debugging by creating higher-fidelity logs for Pro/Enterprise users. This isolates where your code is breaking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design an automated error tracking system that triggers higher logging verbosity for users on paid subscription plans.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'implement tiered logging wrapper around existing error handler'

steps.title: Refactor Monolithic Logic into Micro-Services
steps.description: Break down the 'un-debuggable' parts of your app into isolated services corresponding to your priced features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my monolithic controller into modular services to align with my new pricing tiers, ensuring each module is independently testable.

steps.title: Validate Pricing Logic via Unit Tests
steps.description: Ensure your pricing logic is bug-free by writing tests that simulate user plan upgrades and downgrades.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of Jest unit tests to verify that feature access correctly toggles based on mock subscription tier changes.

pro_tips: Always build the 'Free' tier as a completely separate, simplified codebase path to avoid accidental feature leaks.
Use database-level constraints for subscription checks; never rely solely on client-side UI hiding for security.
Implement a 'Debug Mode' environment variable that bypasses pricing gates only in local development to speed up iteration.
If you cannot debug a feature, it is likely too complex; simplify the feature until it fits within a single pricing tier.
Leverage Claude Code to write documentation for your API endpoints as you build them, preventing 'documentation debt' later.
content.next_steps: After refining your pricing logic, deploy your application to a staging environment and perform a 'Plan Upgrade' simulation to ensure your feature gates hold up under load.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start architecting a business. By aligning your technical constraints with your pricing model, you build more reliable, scalable software. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
